<br clear="all" style="page-break-before:always;">


<div>

<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><b><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-weight:bold;">Item 7.&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160; Management&#146;s Discussion and
Analysis of Financial Condition and Results of Operations.</font></b><a name="Item7_ManagementsDiscussionAndAna_181344"></a></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">The following discussion
and analysis should be read in conjunction with our accompanying consolidated
financial statements and the notes thereto:</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;text-autospace:none;text-indent:0in;"><b><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-weight:bold;">Critical Accounting Policies and Estimates</font></b></p>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;text-autospace:none;text-indent:0in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">Management&#146;s discussion
and analysis of financial condition and results of operations are based upon
our consolidated financial statements, which have been prepared in accordance
with accounting principles generally accepted in the United States of America (&#147;GAAP&#148;).&#160; The preparation of these financial statements
requires our management to make estimates and assumptions that affect the
reported amounts of assets, liabilities, revenues and expenses, and related
disclosure of contingent assets and liabilities.&#160; On a regular basis, we will evaluate these
estimates.&#160; These estimates will be based
on management&#146;s historical industry experience and on various other assumptions
that are believed to be reasonable under the circumstances.&#160; Actual results may differ from these
estimates.</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in .05in .0001pt .25in;"><b><i><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-style:italic;font-weight:bold;">Principles
of Consolidation and Basis of Presentation</font></i></b></p>

<p style="margin:0in .05in .0001pt .25in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">The consolidated
financial statements include our accounts and the accounts of our
subsidiaries.&#160; All inter-company
transactions, balances and profits have been eliminated in consolidation.&#160; Interests in entities acquired are evaluated
based on Financial Accounting Standards Board Interpretation (&#147;FIN&#148;) No.&nbsp;46R
&#147;Consolidation of Variable Interest Entities,&#148; which requires the consolidation
of variable interest entities (&#147;VIE&#148;) in which we are deemed to be the primary
beneficiary.&#160; If the interest in the
entity is determined not to be a VIE under FIN No.&nbsp;46R, then the entities
are evaluated for consolidation under the American Institute of Certified
Public Accountants (&#147;AICPA&#148;) Statement of Position (&#147;SOP&#148;) 78-9 &#147;Accounting for
Investments in Real Estate Ventures,&#148; as amended by Emerging Issues Task Force
(&#147;EITF&#148;) Issue 04-5, &#147;Determining Whether a General Partner, or the General
Partners as a Group, Controls a Limited Partnership or Similar Entity When the
Limited Partners Have Certain Rights.&#148;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.25in;"><b><i><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-style:italic;font-weight:bold;">Real
Estate</font></i></b></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.25in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">Upon the acquisition of
real estate properties, we allocate the purchase price of those properties to
the tangible assets acquired, consisting of land, inclusive of associated
rights, and buildings, identified intangible assets, asset retirement
obligations and assumed liabilities based on their relative fair values in
accordance with Statement of Financial Accounting Standards No.&nbsp;142, &#147;Goodwill
and Other Intangible Assets.&#148;&#160; Identified
intangible assets consist of the fair value of above-market and below-market
leases, in-place leases, in-place tenant improvements and tenant
relationships.&#160; Initial valuations are
subject to change until our information is finalized, which is no later than 12
months from the acquisition date.</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">The fair value of the
tangible assets acquired, consisting of land and buildings, is determined by
valuing the property as if it were vacant, and the &#147;as-if-vacant&#148; value is then
allocated to land and buildings.&#160; Land
values are derived from appraisals, and building values are calculated as
replacement cost less depreciation or management&#146;s estimates of the relative
fair value of these assets using discounted cash flow analyses or similar
methods.&#160; The value of buildings is
depreciated over the estimated useful life of 25 years using the straight-line
method.</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">We determine the value of
above-market and below-market in-place leases for acquired properties based on
the present value (using an interest rate that reflects the risks associated
with the leases acquired) of the difference between (1)&nbsp;the contractual
amounts to be paid pursuant to the in-place leases and (2)&nbsp;management&#146;s
estimate of current market lease rates for the corresponding in-place leases,
measured over a period equal to the determined lease term.&#160; We record the fair value of above-market and
below-market leases as intangible assets or intangible liabilities,
respectively, and amortize them as an adjustment to rental income over the
determined lease term.</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">The total value of
identified real estate intangible assets acquired is further allocated to
in-place lease values, in-place tenant improvements, in-place leasing
commissions and tenant relationships based on management&#146;s evaluation of the
specific characteristics of each tenant&#146;s lease and our overall relationship
with that respective tenant.&#160; The
aggregate value for tenant improvements and leasing commissions is based on
estimates of these costs incurred at inception of the acquired leases,
amortized through the date of acquisition.&#160;
The aggregate value of in-place leases acquired and tenant relationships
is determined by applying a fair value model.&#160;
The estimates of the fair value of in-place leases includes an estimate
of carrying costs during the expected lease-up periods for the respective
spaces, considering current market conditions.&#160;
In estimating the carrying costs that would have otherwise been incurred
had the leases not been in place, management includes such items as real estate
taxes, insurance and other operating expenses as well as lost rental revenue
during the expected lease-up period based on current market conditions.&#160; The estimates of fair value of tenant
relationships also include costs to execute similar leases including leasing
commissions, legal and tenant improvements, as well as an estimate of the
likelihood of renewal as determined by management on a tenant-by-tenant basis.</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">We amortize the value of
in-place leases and in-place tenant improvements to expense over the initial
term of the respective leases.&#160; The value
of tenant relationship intangibles is amortized to expense over the initial
term and any </font></p>

<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">32</font></p>

<div style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><hr size="3" width="100%" noshade color="#010101" align="left"></div>

</div>
<!-- SEQ.=1,FOLIO='32',FILE='C:\JMS\jnewcoma\08-2957-1\task2825997\2957-1-ba-05.htm',USER='jnewcoma',CD='Mar 31 09:13 2008' -->


<br clear="all" style="page-break-before:always;">


<div style="font-family:Times New Roman;">

<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">anticipated renewal
periods, but in no event does the amortization period for intangible assets
exceed the remaining depreciable life of the building.&#160; Should a tenant terminate its lease, the
unamortized portion of the related real estate intangibles would be charged to
expense.</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">In allocating the
purchase price of each of our properties, management makes assumptions and uses
various estimates, including, but not limited to, the estimated useful lives of
the assets, the cost of replacing certain assets, discount rates used to
determine present values, market rental rates per square foot and the period
required to lease the property up to its occupancy at acquisition if it were
vacant.&#160; Many of these estimates are
obtained from independent third party appraisals.&#160; However, management is responsible for the
source and use of these estimates.&#160; A
change in these estimates and assumptions could result in the various
categories of our real estate assets and/or related intangibles being
overstated or understated, which could result in an overstatement or
understatement of depreciation and/or amortization expense.&#160; These variances could be material to our
financial statements.</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.25in;"><b><i><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-style:italic;font-weight:bold;">Impairment
of Long-Lived Assets</font></i></b></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.25in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">For real estate we wholly
own, management monitors events and changes in circumstances indicating that
the carrying amounts of the real estate assets may not be recoverable.&#160; When such events or changes in circumstances
occur, we assess potential impairment by comparing estimated future
undiscounted operating cash flows expected to be generated over the life of the
asset, including its eventual disposition, to the carrying amount of the
asset.&#160; In the event that the carrying
amount exceeds the estimated future undiscounted operating cash flows, we
perform an impairment analysis to determine if the carrying amount of the asset
needs to be reduced to estimated fair value.&#160;
We determine the estimated fair value based on cash flow streams using
various factors including estimated future selling prices, costs spent to date,
remaining budgeted costs and selling costs.</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">During 2007, the housing
market experienced difficult conditions including high inventory levels,
tightening of the credit market and weak consumer confidence.&#160; In the event that market conditions continue
to decline in the future or the current difficult market conditions extend
beyond our expectations, impairments may be necessary in the future.&#160; There were no impairment charges for
long-lived assets for the years ended December&nbsp;31, 2007, 2006 or 2005.</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">In evaluating our
investments for impairment, management makes several estimates and assumptions,
including, but not limited to, the projected date of disposition of the
properties, the estimated future cash flows of the properties during our
ownership and the projected sales price of each of the properties.&#160; A change in these estimates and assumptions
could result in understating or overstating the book value of our investments,
which could be material to our financial statements.</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><b><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-weight:bold;">Overview</font></b></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">We are organized as a
Texas limited partnership formed primarily to invest in and operate
institutional quality office and other commercial properties, including
properties that have been constructed and have operating histories, are newly
constructed or are under development or construction.</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">We purchased our first
property on March&nbsp;12, 2004 in Minneapolis, Minnesota.&#160; As of December&nbsp;31, 2007, we wholly-owned
five properties.&#160; These properties
combined contain approximately 236,000 rentable square feet.</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><b><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-weight:bold;">Results of Operations</font></b></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><i><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-style:italic;">Fiscal year ended December&nbsp;31,
2007 as compared to fiscal year ended December&nbsp;31, 2006</font></i></p>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;text-autospace:none;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;text-autospace:none;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">As of December&nbsp;31,
2007 and 2006, we wholly-owned five properties.&#160;
The operations of the Northpoint Property, which was sold on December&nbsp;28,
2006, is classified as discontinued operations in each of the years presented
in the accompanying consolidated statements of operations.</font></p>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;text-autospace:none;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt .25in;text-align:left;text-autospace:none;text-indent:0in;"><b><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-weight:bold;">Continuing Operations</font></b></p>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt .25in;text-align:left;text-autospace:none;text-indent:0in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><i><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-style:italic;">Rental
Revenue.&#160; </font></i><font size="2" style="font-size:10.0pt;">Rental revenue for the years ended December&nbsp;31,
2007 and 2006 was $4,128,395 and $4,253,765, respectively, and was comprised of
revenue, including adjustments for straight-line rent and amortization of
above-market and below-market leases, from our properties.&#160; Management expects rental revenue to remain
relatively constant in the near future.</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><i><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-style:italic;">Property
Operating Expenses.&#160; </font></i><font size="2" style="font-size:10.0pt;">Property operating expenses for the years
ended December&nbsp;31, 2007 and 2006 were $793,857 and $744,210, respectively,
and were comprised of expenses related to the daily operations from our
properties.&#160; The increase in property
operating expenses for the year ended December&nbsp;31, 2007 is primarily due
to non-recurring repairs and maintenance expenses.&#160; We expect property operating expenses to
remain relatively constant in the near future.</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in .05in .0001pt 0in;text-indent:.5in;"><i><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-style:italic;">Real
Estate Taxes.&#160; </font></i><font size="2" style="font-size:10.0pt;">Real estate taxes for the years ended December&nbsp;31,
2007 and 2006 were $681,720 and $782,803, respectively, and were comprised of
real estate taxes from our properties.&#160;
The decrease in real estate taxes for </font></p>

<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">33</font></p>

<br clear="all" style="page-break-before:always;">


<div style="font-family:Times New Roman;">

<p style="margin:0in .05in .0001pt 0in;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">the year ended December&nbsp;31,
2007 is primarily due to successful appeals of property tax assessments.&#160; We expect real estate taxes&#160; to remain relatively constant in the near
future.</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in .05in .0001pt 0in;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in .05in .0001pt 0in;text-indent:.5in;"><i><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-style:italic;">Property
and Asset Management Fees.&#160; </font></i><font size="2" style="font-size:10.0pt;">Property and asset management fees for
the years ended December&nbsp;31, 2007 and 2006 were $297,909 and $293,742,
respectively, and were comprised of property management and asset management
fees from our properties.&#160; Management
expects property and asset management fees to remain relatively constant in the
near future.</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in .05in .0001pt 0in;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in .05in .0001pt 0in;text-indent:.5in;"><i><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-style:italic;">General
and Administrative Expenses.&#160; </font></i><font size="2" style="font-size:10.0pt;">General and administrative expenses for
the years ended December&nbsp;31, 2007 and 2006 were $408,355 and $509,390,
respectively, and were comprised of corporate general and administrative
expenses, including auditing fees, directors&#146; and officers&#146; insurance premiums,
tax preparation fees, legal fees, transfer agent fees, Sarbanes-Oxley advisory
services, printing costs and other administrative expenses.&#160; The decrease is primarily due to a reduction
in audit fees and legal fees.&#160; Management
expects future general and administrative expenses to remain relatively
constant.</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in .05in .0001pt 0in;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in .05in .0001pt 0in;text-indent:.5in;"><i><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-style:italic;">Depreciation
and Amortization Expense.&#160; </font></i><font size="2" style="font-size:10.0pt;">Depreciation and amortization expense for
the years ended December&nbsp;31, 2007 and 2006 was $1,599,838 and $1,569,492,
respectively, and was comprised of depreciation and amortization of our
properties.&#160; Management expects
depreciation and amortization expenses to remain relatively constant in the
near future.</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in .05in .0001pt 0in;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in .05in .0001pt 0in;text-indent:.5in;"><i><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-style:italic;">Interest
Income. </font></i><font size="2" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&#160;Interest income for the year ended December&nbsp;31,
2007 was $368,033 and was comprised primarily of interest income associated
with monies that were held in an escrow account from the sale of the Northpoint
Property.&#160; Interest income for the year
ended December&nbsp;31, 2006 was $41,582 and was comprised primarily of
interest income from funds on deposit with banks.</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in .05in .0001pt 0in;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in .05in .0001pt 0in;text-indent:.5in;"><i><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-style:italic;">Gain on
Sale of Asset.</font></i><font size="2" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&#160; We recognized a gain on the sale of asset for
the year ended December&nbsp;31, 2007 of $50,570.&#160; The gain was a result of the sale of a parcel
of land at 2800 W. Mockingbird on September&nbsp;5, 2007.&#160; No gain on sale of assets was recognized
during the year ended December&nbsp;31, 2006.</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in .05in .0001pt .25in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in .05in .0001pt .25in;"><b><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-weight:bold;">Discontinued
Operations</font></b></p>

<p style="margin:0in .05in .0001pt .25in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in .05in .0001pt 0in;text-indent:.5in;"><i><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-style:italic;">Income
from Discontinued Operations.</font></i><font size="2" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&#160; The income
from discontinued operations for the years ended December&nbsp;31, 2007 and
2006 was $4,503 and $119,980, respectively.&#160;
The income from discontinued operations for the respective periods
represents the activity for the Northpoint Property, which was sold on December&nbsp;28,
2006.&#160; The results for the year ended December&nbsp;31,
2007 represent the final settlements for operations of the Northpoint Property.</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in .05in .0001pt 0in;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in .05in .0001pt 0in;text-indent:.5in;"><i><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-style:italic;">Gain on
Sale of Discontinued Operations.</font></i><font size="2" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&#160; There was no
gain on sale of discontinued operations for the year ended December&nbsp;31,
2007<i>.</i>&#160;
We recognized a gain on the sale of discontinued operations for the year
ended December&nbsp;31, 2006 of $494,195, as a result of the sale of the
Northpoint Property on December&nbsp;28, 2006.</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in .05in .0001pt 0in;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><i><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-style:italic;">Fiscal year ended December&nbsp;31,
2006 as compared to fiscal year ended December&nbsp;31, 2005</font></i></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;text-autospace:none;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">As of December&nbsp;31,
2006 and 2005, we wholly-owned five and six properties, respectively.&#160; The Northpoint Property was sold on December&nbsp;28,
2006 and was classified as discontinued operations.&#160; We purchased 2800&nbsp;W. Mockingbird on March&nbsp;11,
2005, Parkway Vista on June&nbsp;8, 2005 and the ASC Building on December&nbsp;21,
2005.&#160; As a result, our results of
operations for the year ended December&nbsp;31, 2006 are not comparable to
results for the year ended December&nbsp;31, 2005.</font></p>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;text-autospace:none;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt .25in;text-align:left;text-autospace:none;text-indent:0in;"><b><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-weight:bold;">Continuing Operations</font></b></p>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt .25in;text-align:left;text-autospace:none;text-indent:0in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><i><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-style:italic;">Rental
Revenue.&#160; </font></i><font size="2" style="font-size:10.0pt;">Rental revenue for the years ended December&nbsp;31,
2006 and 2005 was $4,253,765 and $2,955,742, respectively, and was comprised of
revenue, including adjustments for straight-line rent and amortization of
above-market and below-market leases, from our properties.&#160; The increase in revenue for the year ended December&nbsp;31,
2006 was primarily due to the additional revenue as a result of recognizing a
full year of revenues from properties acquired during 2005.</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><i><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-style:italic;">Property
Operating Expenses.&#160; </font></i><font size="2" style="font-size:10.0pt;">Property operating expenses for the years
ended December&nbsp;31, 2006 and 2005 were $744,210 and $527,950, respectively,
and were comprised of expenses related to the daily operations from our
properties.&#160; The increase in property
operating expenses for the year ended December&nbsp;31, 2006 was primarily due
to recognizing a full year of operations from properties acquired during 2005.</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in .05in .0001pt 0in;text-indent:.5in;"><i><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-style:italic;">Real
Estate Taxes.&#160; </font></i><font size="2" style="font-size:10.0pt;">Real estate taxes for the years ended December&nbsp;31,
2006 and 2005 were $782,803 and $459,476, respectively, and were comprised of
real estate taxes from our properties.&#160;
The increase in real estate taxes for the year ended December&nbsp;31,
2006 was primarily due to an increase in our portfolio.</font></p>

<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">34</font></p>

<div style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><hr size="3" width="100%" noshade color="#010101" align="left"></div>

<br clear="all" style="page-break-before:always;">


<div style="font-family:Times New Roman;">

<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in .05in .0001pt 0in;text-indent:.5in;"><i><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-style:italic;">Property
and Asset Management Fees.&#160; </font></i><font size="2" style="font-size:10.0pt;">Property and asset management fees for
the years ended December&nbsp;31, 2006 and 2005 were $293,742 and $209,898,
respectively, and were comprised of property management and asset management
fees from our properties.&#160; The increase
in property and asset management fees for the year ended December&nbsp;31, 2006
was primarily due to recognizing a full year of management fees from properties
acquired during 2005.</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in .05in .0001pt 0in;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in .05in .0001pt 0in;text-indent:.5in;"><i><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-style:italic;">General
and Administrative Expenses.&#160; </font></i><font size="2" style="font-size:10.0pt;">General and administrative expenses for
the years ended December&nbsp;31, 2006 and 2005 were $509,390 and $609,986,
respectively, and were comprised of corporate general and administrative
expenses, including auditing fees, directors&#146; and officers&#146; insurance premiums,
organization expenses, transfer agent fees, Sarbanes-Oxley advisory services,
printing costs and other administrative expenses.&#160; The decrease was primarily due to a reduction
in audit fees.</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in .05in .0001pt 0in;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in .05in .0001pt 0in;text-indent:.5in;"><i><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-style:italic;">Depreciation
and Amortization Expense.&#160; </font></i><font size="2" style="font-size:10.0pt;">Depreciation and amortization expense for
the years ended December&nbsp;31, 2006 and 2005 was $1,569,492 and $1,046,664,
respectively, and was comprised of depreciation and amortization of our
properties.&#160; The increase in depreciation
and amortization expense for the year ended December&nbsp;31, 2006 was
primarily due to recognizing a full year of expense from properties acquired
during 2005.</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in .05in .0001pt 0in;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in .05in .0001pt 0in;text-indent:.5in;"><i><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-style:italic;">Interest
Income. </font></i><font size="2" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&#160;Interest income for the years ended December&nbsp;31,
2006 and 2005 was $41,582 and $214,002, respectively, and was comprised
primarily of interest income associated with funds on deposit with banks.&#160; The decrease in interest income was due to
lower cash balances on deposit with banks as a result of the termination of the
Offering on February&nbsp;19, 2005 and the use of proceeds from the Offering to
purchase three additional properties during 2005.</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in .05in .0001pt 0in;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in .05in .0001pt .25in;"><b><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-weight:bold;">Discontinued
Operations</font></b></p>

<p style="margin:0in .05in .0001pt .25in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in .05in .0001pt 0in;text-indent:.5in;"><i><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-style:italic;">Income
from Discontinued Operations.</font></i><font size="2" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&#160; The income
from discontinued operations for the years ended December&nbsp;31, 2006 and
2005 was $119,980 and $126,560, respectively.&#160;
The income from discontinued operations for the respective periods
represents the activity for the Northpoint Property, which was sold on December&nbsp;28,
2006.</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in .05in .0001pt 0in;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in .05in .0001pt 0in;text-indent:.5in;"><i><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-style:italic;">Gain on
Sale of Discontinued Operations.</font></i><font size="2" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&#160; We recognized
a gain on the sale of discontinued operations for the year ended December&nbsp;31,
2006 of $494,195, as a result of the sale of the Northpoint Property on December&nbsp;28,
2006.&#160; There was no gain on sale of
discontinued operations for the year ended December&nbsp;31, 2005.</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in .05in .0001pt 0in;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><b><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-weight:bold;">Cash Flow Analysis</font></b></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">As of December&nbsp;31,
2007 and 2006, we wholly-owned five properties.&#160;
At December&nbsp;31, 2005, we wholly-owned six properties, including the
Northpoint Property, which was sold on December&nbsp;28, 2006.&#160; As a result, our cash flows for the years
ended December&nbsp;31, 2007 and December&nbsp;31, 2006 are not comparable to
the cash flows for the year ended December&nbsp;31, 2005.</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;text-autospace:none;text-indent:0in;"><i><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-style:italic;">Fiscal year ended December&nbsp;31, 2007 as
compared to the fiscal year ended December&nbsp;31, 2006</font></i></p>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;text-autospace:none;text-indent:0in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">Cash provided by
operating activities for the year ended December&nbsp;31, 2007 was $1,864,724
and was primarily comprised of the net income of $756,130, adjusted for
depreciation and amortization of $1,574,226 offset primarily by changes in
working capital accounts of $373,802.&#160;
Cash provided by operating activities for the year ended December&nbsp;31,
2006 was $2,866,196 and was primarily comprised of the net income of
$1,009,885, adjusted for depreciation and amortization of $1,854,071, changes
in working capital accounts of $520,660 and offset primarily by the gain on
sale of discontinued operations of $494,195.</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">Cash provided by
investing activities for the year ended December&nbsp;31, 2007 was $5,968,093
and was comprised of the change in restricted cash of $5,895,192 from the sale
of the Northpoint Property, proceeds from the sale of asset of $93,917 and
offset by the purchases of property and equipment of $21,016.&#160; Cash provided by investing activities for the
year ended December&nbsp;31, 2006 was $82,317 and was comprised of proceeds
from our sale of discontinued operations of $6,099,022, offset by the change in
restricted cash of $5,895,192 and the purchases of property and equipment of
$121,513.</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">Cash used in financing
activities for the years ended December&nbsp;31, 2007 and 2006 was $2,862,057
and $3,337,609, respectively.&#160; The
decrease is primarily due to the termination of the Redemption Program in 2006.</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><i><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-style:italic;">Fiscal year ended December&nbsp;31,
2006 as compared to the fiscal year ended December&nbsp;31, 2005</font></i></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">Cash provided by
operating activities for the year ended December&nbsp;31, 2006 was $2,866,196
and was primarily comprised of the net income of $1,009,885, adjusted for
depreciation and amortization of $1,854,071, changes in working capital
accounts of $520,660 and offset primarily by the gain on sale of discontinued
operations of $494,195.&#160; Cash provided by
operating activities for the year ended December&nbsp;31, 2005 was $1,715,622
and was comprised of the net income of $442,330, adjusted for depreciation and
amortization of $1,366,839 and offset by changes in working capital accounts of
$93,547.</font></p>

<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">35</font></p>

<br clear="all" style="page-break-before:always;">


<div>

<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">Cash provided by
investing activities for the year ended December&nbsp;31, 2006 was $82,317 and
was comprised of proceeds from our sale of discontinued operations of
$6,099,022, offset by the change in restricted cash of $5,895,192 and the
purchases of property and equipment of $121,513.&#160; Cash used in investing activities for the
year ended December&nbsp;31, 2005 was $16,421,254 and was comprised primarily
of our acquisitions of 2800 W. Mockingbird, Parkway Vista and the ASC Building.</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">Cash used in financing
activities for the year ended December&nbsp;31, 2006 was $3,337,609 and was
comprised of our distributions of $2,630,730 and redemptions of $706,879.&#160; Cash provided by financing activities for the
year ended December&nbsp;31, 2005 was $8,539,932.&#160; The decrease is primarily due to the
termination of the offering of partnership units in 2005 and increased
redemptions in 2006.</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><b><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-weight:bold;">Liquidity and Capital Resources</font></b></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">Our cash and cash
equivalents were $7,613,543 and $2,642,783 at December&nbsp;31, 2007 and 2006,
respectively.&#160; The increase in cash is
primarily related to the proceeds from the sale of the Northpoint Property.</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">Our principal demands for
funds will be for the payment of operating expenses and distributions.&#160; Generally, cash needs are expected to be met
from operations. &#160;The timing and amount
of cash to be distributed to our limited partners is determined by the General
Partners and is dependent on a number of factors, including funds available for
payment of distributions, financial condition and capital expenditures.&#160; There can be no assurance that future cash flow
will support distributions at the current rate.&#160;
We expect to continue to distribute net cash from operations and
non-liquidating sales of properties to limited partners.&#160; However, our General Partners, in their
discretion, may defer fees payable by us to the General Partners, allowing for
more cash to be available to us for distribution to our limited partners.&#160; In addition, our General Partners may make
supplemental payments to us or to our limited partners, or otherwise support
our operations to the extent not prohibited under the NASAA Guidelines, which
would permit distributions to our limited partners in excess of net cash from
operations.</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">Recently, the U.S. credit
markets and the sub-prime residential mortgage market have experienced severe
dislocations and liquidity disruptions.&#160;
Sub-prime mortgage loans have experienced increasing rates of
delinquency, foreclosure and loss.&#160; These
and other related events have had a significant impact on the capital markets
associated not only with sub-prime mortgage-backed securities, asset-backed
securities and collateralized debt obligations, but also with the U.S. credit
and financial markets as a whole.&#160;
Consequently, there is greater uncertainty regarding our ability to
access the credit market in order to attract financing on reasonable terms.&#160; Our ability to borrow funds could be adversely
affected by our inability to secure permanent financing on reasonable terms, if
at all.</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">We expect to meet our
future short-term operating liquidity requirements through net cash provided by
the operations of current properties.&#160;
Management also expects that our properties will generate sufficient
cash flow to cover operating expenses and the payment of a monthly
distribution.&#160; Currently, distributions
are paid from available cash on hand.&#160;
Other potential future sources of capital include proceeds from secured
or unsecured financings from banks or other lenders, proceeds from the sale of
properties and undistributed funds from operations.&#160; If necessary, we may use financings or other
sources of capital in the event of unforeseen significant capital expenditures.</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><b><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-weight:bold;">Net Operating Income</font></b></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">Net operating income (&#147;NOI&#148;)
is a non-GAAP financial measure that is defined as net income, computed in
accordance with GAAP, generated from properties before interest expense, asset
management fees, interest income, general and administrative expenses,
depreciation, amortization, income taxes and the sale of assets.&#160; Management believes that NOI provides an
accurate measure of our operating performance because NOI reflects the operating
performance of our properties and excludes certain items that are not
associated with management of the properties.&#160;
To facilitate understanding of this financial measure, a reconciliation
of NOI to net income has been provided in accordance with GAAP.&#160; Our calculations of NOI for the years ended December&nbsp;31,
2007, 2006 and 2005 are presented below:</font></p>

<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">36</font></p>

<div style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><hr size="3" width="100%" noshade color="#010101" align="left"></div>

<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><b><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-weight:bold;">Performance
Reporting Required by the Partnership Agreement</font></b></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">Section 15.2 in our
Partnership Agreement requires us to provide our limited partners with our net
cash from operations, a non-GAAP financial measure which is defined as net
income, computed in accordance with GAAP, plus depreciation and amortization on
real estate assets and adjustments for gain from sale of asset, gain on sale of
discontinued operations and capital improvements (&#147;Net Cash From Operations&#148;).&#160; Our calculations of Net Cash From Operations
for the years ended December&nbsp;31, 2007 and 2006 are presented below:</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><b><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-weight:bold;">Off-Balance Sheet Arrangements</font></b></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">We have no off-balance
sheet arrangements that are reasonably likely to have a current or future
material effect on our financial condition, changes in financial condition,
revenues or expenses, results of operations, liquidity, capital expenditures or
capital resources.</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><b><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-weight:bold;">Contractual Obligations</font></b></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">We had no contractual
obligations as of December&nbsp;31, 2007.</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><b><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-weight:bold;">&nbsp;</font></b></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><b><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-weight:bold;">New Accounting Pronouncements</font></b></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">In September&nbsp;2006,
the Financial Accounting Standards Board (&#147;FASB&#148;) issued Statement of Financial
Accounting Standards (&#147;SFAS&#148;) No.&nbsp;157, &#147;Fair Value Measurements.&#148;&#160; SFAS No.&nbsp;157 establishes a single
authoritative definition of fair value, sets out a framework for measuring fair
value, and requires additional disclosures about fair value measurements.&#160; SFAS No.&nbsp;157 applies only to fair value
measurements that are already required or permitted by other</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">37</font></p>

<div style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><hr size="3" width="100%" noshade color="#010101" align="left"></div>

</div>
<!-- SEQ.=1,FOLIO='37',FILE='C:\JMS\jnewcoma\08-2957-1\task2825997\2957-1-ba-05.htm',USER='jnewcoma',CD='Mar 31 09:13 2008' -->


<br clear="all" style="page-break-before:always;">


<div style="font-family:Times New Roman;">

<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">accounting
standards.&#160; SFAS No.&nbsp;157 is
effective for fiscal years beginning after November&nbsp;15, 2007.&#160; We are in the process of determining the
effect of the adoption that SFAS No.&nbsp;157 will have on our financial
statements.</font></p>

<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">In February&nbsp;2007,
the FASB issued SFAS No.&nbsp;159, &#147;The Fair Value Option for Financial Assets
and Financial Liabilities&nbsp;&#151; Including an Amendment of FASB Statement No.&nbsp;115.&#148;&#160; The fair value option established by SFAS No.&nbsp;159
permits all entities to choose to measure eligible items at fair value at
specified election dates.&#160; A business
entity will report unrealized gains and losses on items for which the fair
value option has been elected in earnings (or another performance indicator if
the business entity does not report earnings) at each subsequent reporting
date.&#160; SFAS No.&nbsp;159 is effective for
fiscal years beginning after November&nbsp;15, 2007.&#160; We have not elected the fair value
measurement option for any financial assets or liabilities at the present time.</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">In December&nbsp;2007,
the FASB issued SFAS No.&nbsp;141(R), &#147;Business
Combinations.&#148;&nbsp; This Statement replaces SFAS No.&nbsp;141,
&#147;Business Combinations&#148; but retains the fundamental requirement that the
acquisition method of accounting, or&#160;
purchase method, be used for all business combinations and for an
acquirer to be identified for each business combination.&#160; This Statement is broader in scope than that
of SFAS No. 141, which applied only to business combinations in which control
was obtained by transferring consideration.&#160;
SFAS No.&nbsp;141(R) applies the same method of accounting (the
acquisition method) to all transactions and other events in which one entity
obtains control over one or more other businesses.&nbsp; This Statement also
makes certain other modifications to SFAS No. 141, including a broader
definition of a business and the requirement that acquisition related costs are
expensed as incurred.&nbsp; This Statement applies to business combinations
occurring on or after the beginning of the first annual reporting period
beginning on or after December&nbsp;15, 2008.&#160;
Early adoption is not allowed.&#160; We
are currently assessing the effect SFAS No.&nbsp;141(R)&nbsp;may have on our
consolidated results of operations and financial position.</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in .05in .0001pt 0in;"><b><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-weight:bold;">Inflation</font></b></p>

<p style="margin:0in .05in .0001pt 0in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">The real estate market
has not been affected significantly by inflation in the past several years due
to the relatively low inflation rate.&#160;
The majority of our leases contain inflation protection provisions
applicable to reimbursement billings for common area maintenance charges, real
estate tax and insurance reimbursements on a per square foot basis, or in some
cases, annual reimbursement of operating expenses above a certain per square
foot allowance.</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt 1.0in;text-indent:-1.0in;"><b><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-weight:bold;">Item 7A.</font></b><b><font size="1" style="font-size:3.0pt;font-weight:bold;">&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160; </font></b><b><font size="2" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-weight:bold;">Quantitative and Qualitative
Disclosures About Market Risk.</font></b><a name="Item7a_QuantitativeAndQualitative_181401"></a></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">We have limited exposure
to financial market risks, including changes in interest rates and other
relevant market prices.&#160; We have no
investments or obligations that would be affected by an increase or decrease in
interest rates.&#160; At December&nbsp;31,
2007, we did not have any foreign operations and thus were not exposed to
foreign currency fluctuations.</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt 1.0in;text-indent:-1.0in;"><b><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-weight:bold;">Item 8.</font></b><b><font size="1" style="font-size:3.0pt;font-weight:bold;">&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160; </font></b><b><font size="2" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-weight:bold;">Financial Statements and
Supplementary Data.</font></b><a name="Item8_FinancialStatementsAndSuppl_181404"></a></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">The information required
by this Item 8 is provided in our Financial Statements beginning on page&nbsp;F-1
of this Annual Report on Form&nbsp;10-K.</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt 1.0in;text-indent:-1.0in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt 1.0in;text-indent:-1.0in;"><b><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-weight:bold;">Item 9.</font></b><b><font size="1" style="font-size:3.0pt;font-weight:bold;">&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160; </font></b><b><font size="2" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-weight:bold;">Changes in and Disagreements With
Accountants on Accounting and Financial Disclosure.</font></b><a name="Item9_ChangesInAndDisagreementsWi_181412"></a></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt 1.0in;text-indent:-1.0in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">None.</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt 1.0in;text-indent:-1.0in;"><b><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-weight:bold;">Item 9A(T).</font></b><b><font size="1" style="font-size:3.0pt;font-weight:bold;">&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160; </font></b><b><font size="2" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-weight:bold;">Controls and Procedures.</font></b><a name="Item9at_ControlsAndProcedures__181416"></a></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><i><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-style:italic;">Evaluation
of Disclosure Controls and Procedures</font></i></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">As required by Rule&nbsp;13a-15(b)&nbsp;and
Rule&nbsp;15d-15(b)&nbsp;under the Exchange Act, the management of Behringer
Advisors I, including its Chief Executive Officer and Chief Financial Officer,
evaluated as of December&nbsp;31, 2007 the effectiveness of our disclosure
controls and procedures as defined in Exchange Act Rule&nbsp;13a-15(e)&nbsp;and
Rule&nbsp;15d-15(e). Based on that evaluation, the Chief Executive Officer and
Chief Financial Officer of Behringer Advisors I concluded that our disclosure
controls and procedures, as of December&nbsp;31, 2007, were effective for the
purpose of ensuring that information required to be disclosed by us in this
report is recorded, processed, summarized and reported within the time periods
specified by the rules&nbsp;and forms of the Exchange Act and is accumulated
and communicated to the management of Behringer Advisors I, including its Chief
Executive Officer and Chief Financial Officer, as appropriate to allow timely
decisions regarding required disclosures.</font></p>

<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">38</font></p>

<div style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><hr size="3" width="100%" noshade color="#010101" align="left"></div>

</div>
<!-- SEQ.=1,FOLIO='38',FILE='C:\JMS\jnewcoma\08-2957-1\task2825997\2957-1-ba-05.htm',USER='jnewcoma',CD='Mar 31 09:13 2008' -->


<br clear="all" style="page-break-before:always;">


<div style="font-family:Times New Roman;">

<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">We believe, however, that
a controls system, no matter how well designed and operated, cannot provide
absolute assurance that the objectives of the controls systems are met, and no
evaluation of controls can provide absolute assurance that all control issues
and instances of fraud or error, if any, within a partnership have been
detected.</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><i><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-style:italic;">Management&#146;s
Annual Report on Internal Control over Financial Reporting</font></i></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">Management is
responsible for establishing and maintaining adequate internal control over
financial reporting (as defined in Exchange Act Rules&nbsp;13a-15(f)&nbsp;and
15d-15(f)).&#160; Our management, including
the Chief Executive Officer and Chief Financial Officer of Behringer Advisors
I, evaluated as of December&nbsp;31, 2007 the effectiveness of our internal
control over financial reporting using the framework in <i>Internal
Control &#150; Integrated Framework </i>issued by the Committee of Sponsoring
Organizations of the Treadway Commission.&#160;
Based on that evaluation, the Chief Executive Officer and Chief
Financial Officer of Behringer Advisors I concluded that our internal controls,
as of December&nbsp;31, 2007, were effective in providing reasonable assurance
regarding reliability of financial reporting.</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">This annual report
does not include an attestation report of the Partnership&#146;s registered public
accounting firm regarding internal control over financial reporting. Management&#146;s
report was not subject to attestation by the Partnership&#146;s registered public
accounting firm pursuant to temporary rules&nbsp;of the Securities and Exchange
Commission that permit the Partnership to provide only management&#146;s report in
this annual report.</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><i><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-style:italic;">Changes
in Internal Control over Financial Reporting</font></i></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">There has been no change
in internal control over financial reporting that occurred during the quarter
ended December&nbsp;31, 2007 that has materially affected, or is reasonably
likely to materially affect, our internal control over financial reporting.</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><b><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-weight:bold;">Item 9B. &#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160; Other
Information.</font></b><a name="Item9b_OtherInformation__181421"></a></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><b><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-weight:bold;">&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160; </font></b><font size="2" style="font-size:10.0pt;">None.</font></p>

<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">39</font></p>

<br clear="all" style="page-break-before:always;">
<div style="font-family:Times New Roman;">

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;"><b><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-weight:bold;">PART&nbsp;III<a name="Partiii_181714"></a></font></b></p>

<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><b><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-weight:bold;">Item 10.&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160; Directors,
Executive Officers and Corporate Governance.</font></b><a name="Item10_DirectorsExecutiveOfficers_181719"></a></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman">&nbsp;</font></p>

<h2 style="font-style:italic;font-weight:bold;margin:0in 0in .0001pt;page-break-after:avoid;"><b><i><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-style:normal;">The General Partners</font></i></b></h2>

<h2 style="font-style:italic;font-weight:bold;margin:0in 0in .0001pt;page-break-after:avoid;"><b><i><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></i></b></h2>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;text-autospace:none;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">We
operate under the direction of our General Partners, which are responsible for
the management and control of our affairs.&#160;
The General Partners are assisted by the employees of HPT, the general
partner of Behringer Advisors I.&#160; In
addition, the General Partners are advised by an advisory board comprised of
industry professionals.&#160; We do not employ
our own management personnel; rather we pay fees to our General Partners for
their services to us.</font></p>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;text-autospace:none;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;text-autospace:none;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">The
General Partners are responsible for our direction and management, including
acquisition, construction and property management.&#160; Any action required to be taken by the
General Partners will be taken only if it is approved, in writing or otherwise,
by both General Partners, unless the General Partners agree between themselves
to a different arrangement for the approval of actions by the General Partners.</font></p>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;text-autospace:none;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;text-autospace:none;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">The
General Partners are Behringer Advisors&nbsp;I and Robert M. Behringer,
individually.&#160; Behringer
Advisors&nbsp;I&nbsp;is a Texas limited partnership formed in July&nbsp;2002.&#160; The executive office of both General Partners
is located at 15601 Dallas Parkway, Suite&nbsp;600, Addison, Texas 75001.&#160; Behringer Advisors&nbsp;I is owned by HPT, its
sole general partner, and Behringer Harvard Partners, LLC (&#147;Behringer Partners&#148;),
its sole limited partner.&#160; Behringer
Holdings is the sole owner of HPT and Behringer Partners.&#160; Mr.&nbsp;Behringer is the President and sole
manager of each of these companies.&#160; Mr.&nbsp;Behringer
is the founder, sole manager and Chief Executive Officer of Behringer
Holdings.&#160; Behringer Holdings also is the
indirect owner of HPT Management, our property manager, Behringer Development,
a real estate development company, and Behringer Securities, our dealer
manager.</font></p>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;text-autospace:none;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;text-autospace:none;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">Behringer
Advisors I was created in 2002 for the sole purpose of acting as one of our
General Partners.&#160; It is managed by its
executive officers, namely:</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

  <p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:1.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
  </td>
  <td width="6%" valign="top" style="padding:0in 0in 0in 0in;width:6.48%;">
  <p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">56</font></p>
  </td>
  <td width="3%" valign="bottom" style="padding:0in 0in 0in 0in;width:3.9%;">
  <p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:1.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
  </td>
  <td width="58%" valign="top" style="padding:0in 0in 0in 0in;width:58.44%;">
  <p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">Executive
  Vice President &#150; Real Estate</font></p>
  </td>

</table>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="font-size:10.0pt;margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><b><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-weight:bold;">Robert M.
Behringer </font></b>is the
Chief Executive Officer and Chief Investment Officer of Behringer
Advisors&nbsp;I.&#160; He is also the Chief
Executive Officer, Chief Investment Officer and Chairman of the Board of
Behringer Harvard REIT&nbsp;I, Behringer Harvard Opportunity REIT I, and
Behringer Harvard Opportunity REIT II, all publicly registered real estate
investment trusts.&#160; Mr.&nbsp;Behringer is
the founder, sole manager and Chief Executive Officer of Behringer Holdings,
the parent of Behringer Advisors I.&#160;
Since 2002, Mr.&nbsp;Behringer has been a general partner of ours and
Behringer Harvard Short-Term Opportunity Fund&nbsp;I, each a publicly
registered real estate limited partnership.&#160;
Mr.&nbsp;Behringer also controls the general partners of Behringer
Harvard Strategic Opportunity Fund I LP and Behringer Harvard Strategic
Opportunity Fund II LP, private real estate limited partnerships.&#160; Since 2001, Mr.&nbsp;Behringer has also been
the Chief Executive Officer of the other Behringer Harvard companies.</p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">From 1995 until 2001, Mr.&nbsp;Behringer was
Chief Executive Officer of Harvard Property Trust,&nbsp;Inc., a privately held
REIT formed by Mr.&nbsp;Behringer that has been liquidated and that had a net
asset value of approximately $200 million before its liquidation.&#160; Before forming Harvard Property Trust,&nbsp;Inc.,
Mr.&nbsp;Behringer invested in commercial real estate as Behringer Partners, a
sole proprietorship formed in 1989 that invested in single asset limited
partnerships.&#160; From 1985 until 1993, Mr.&nbsp;Behringer
was Vice President and Investment Officer of Equitable Real Estate Investment
Management,&nbsp;Inc. (now known as Lend Lease Real Estate Investments,&nbsp;Inc.),
one of the largest pension fund advisors and owners of real estate in the
United States.&#160; While at Equitable, Mr.&nbsp;Behringer
was responsible for its General Account Real Estate Assets located in the south
central United States.&#160; The portfolio
included institutional quality office, industrial, retail, apartment and hotel
properties exceeding 17 million square feet with a value of approximately $2.8
billion.&#160; Although Mr.&nbsp;Behringer was
a significant participant in acquisitions, management, leasing, redevelopment
and dispositions, his primary responsibility was to increase net operating
income and the overall value of the portfolio.</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;text-autospace:none;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">Mr.&nbsp;Behringer
has over 25 years of experience in real estate investment, management and
finance activities, including approximately 140 different properties with over
24 million square feet of office, retail, industrial, apartment, </font></p>

<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;text-autospace:none;text-indent:0in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;text-autospace:none;text-indent:0in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">40</font></p>

<div align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;text-autospace:none;text-indent:0in;"><hr size="3" width="100%" noshade color="#010101" align="left"></div>
