<div style="font-family:Times New Roman;">

<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;text-autospace:none;text-indent:0in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;text-autospace:none;text-indent:0in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">hotel and recreational properties.&#160; Since the founding of the Behringer Harvard
organization, Mr.&nbsp;Behringer&#146;s experience includes an additional 127
properties, with over 29 million square feet of office, retail, industrial,
apartment, hotel and recreational properties.&#160;
In addition to being the Chief Executive Officer and Chief Investment
Officer of Behringer Advisors I, he is currently the general partner or a
co-general partner in several real estate limited partnerships formed for the
purpose of acquiring, developing and operating office buildings and other
commercial properties.&#160; Mr.&nbsp;Behringer
is a Certified Property Manager, Real Property Administrator, Certified Hotel
Administrator and Texas Real Estate Broker, holds FINRA Series&nbsp;7, 24 and
63 registrations and is a member of the Institute of Real Estate Management,
the Building Owners and Managers Association, the Urban Land Institute and the
Real Estate Council.&#160; Mr.&nbsp;Behringer
also was a licensed certified public accountant for over 20 years.&#160; Mr.&nbsp;Behringer received a Bachelor of
Science degree from the University of Minnesota.</font></p>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;text-autospace:none;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="font-size:10.0pt;margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><b><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-weight:bold;">Robert
S. Aisner</font></b> is the President of Behringer

Vice President of AMLI Residential Properties Trust, formerly a New York Stock
Exchange listed REIT that focused on the development, acquisition and
management of upscale apartment communities and served as institutional advisor
and asset manager for institutional investors with respect to their multifamily

Company, which oversees all of AMLI&#146;s apartment operations in 80 communities, (3)&nbsp;President
of the AMLI Corporate Homes division that manages AMLI&#146;s corporate housing
properties, (4)&nbsp;Vice President of AMLI Residential Construction, a
division of AMLI that performs real estate construction services, and (5)&nbsp;Vice

institutional advisor and asset manager for institutional investors with
respect to their multifamily real estate activities.&#160; Mr.&nbsp;Aisner also served on AMLI&#146;s
Executive Committee and Investment Committee from 1999 until 2003.&#160; From 1994 until 1996, Mr.&nbsp;Aisner owned
and operated Regents Management,&nbsp;Inc., which had both a multifamily
development and construction group, and a general commercial property
management company.&#160; From 1984 to 1994,
he was employed by HRW Resources,&nbsp;Inc., a real estate development and
management company, where he served as Vice President.</p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;text-autospace:none;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">Mr.&nbsp;Aisner
served as an independent director of Behringer Harvard REIT I from June&nbsp;2002
until February&nbsp;2003 and as a management director from June&nbsp;2003 until

Behringer Harvard Holdings and President of HPT, IMS, HPT Management and

received a Bachelor of Arts degree from Colby College and a Masters of Business

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;text-autospace:none;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="font-size:10.0pt;margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><b><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-weight:bold;">Gerald J.
Reihsen,&nbsp;III </font></b>is
the Executive Vice President &#150; Corporate Development and Legal and Secretary of
Behringer Advisors&nbsp;I.&#160; Since 2001, Mr.&nbsp;Reihsen
has served in this and similar executive capacities with the other Behringer
Harvard companies, including serving as President of Behringer Securities.</p>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;text-autospace:none;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;text-autospace:none;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">For
over 20 years, Mr.&nbsp;Reihsen&#146;s business and legal background has centered on
sophisticated financial and transactional matters, including commercial real
estate transactions, real estate partnerships, and public and private
securities offerings.&#160; For the period
from 1985 to 2000, Mr.&nbsp;Reihsen practiced as an outside corporate
securities attorney.&#160; After serving from
1986 to 1995 in the corporate department of Gibson, Dunn&nbsp;&amp; Crutcher, a
leading international commercial law firm, Mr.&nbsp;Reihsen established his own
firm, Travis&nbsp;&amp; Reihsen, where he served as a corporate/securities
partner until 1998.&#160; In 1998, Mr.&nbsp;Reihsen
became the lead partner in the corporate/securities section of the law firm
Novakov Davis, where he served until 2000.&#160;
In 2000, he practiced law as a principal of Block&nbsp;&amp; Balestri, a
corporate and securities law firm.&#160; In
2000 and 2001, Mr.&nbsp;Reihsen was employed as the Vice President &#150; Corporate
Development and Legal of Xybridge Technologies,&nbsp;Inc., a telecommunications
software company that Mr.&nbsp;Reihsen helped guide through venture funding,
strategic alliances with international telecommunications leaders and its
ultimate sale to Zhone Technologies,&nbsp;Inc.&#160;
Mr.&nbsp;Reihsen holds FINRA Series&nbsp;7, 24, 27 and 63
registrations.&#160; Mr.&nbsp;Reihsen received
a Bachelor of Arts degree, magna cum laude, from the University of Mississippi
and a Juris Doctorate degree, cum laude, from the University of Wisconsin.</font></p>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;text-autospace:none;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p align="left" style="font-size:10.0pt;margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;text-autospace:none;text-indent:.5in;"><b><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-weight:bold;">Gary S. Bresky </font></b>is the Chief Financial
Officer and Treasurer of Behringer Advisors&nbsp;I.&#160; Mr.&nbsp;Bresky is also the Chief Financial
Officer of all of the other Behringer Harvard companies.</p>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;text-autospace:none;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">Prior to his employment with

Senior Vice President of Finance with Harvard Property Trust,&nbsp;Inc.&#160; In this capacity, Mr.&nbsp;Bresky was
responsible for directing all accounting and financial reporting functions and
overseeing all treasury management and banking functions for the company.&#160; Mr.&nbsp;Bresky was also integral in
analyzing deal and capital structures as well as participating in all major

Lybrand LLP in Dallas, Texas, where he focused on finance and accounting for

experience included conducting annual audits, preparing quarterly and annual

registration statements for his clients.&#160;
From 1989 to 1994, Mr.&nbsp;Bresky worked with Ten West Associates, LTD
and Westwood Financial Corporation in Los Angeles, California as a real estate

Bros.,&nbsp;Inc. and Hambrecht and Quist Inc. assisting brokers in portfolio
management.&#160; Mr.&nbsp;Bresky has been
active in commercial real estate and related financial activities for over 15
years and holds FINRA Series&nbsp;7, 24, 27 and 63 registrations.&#160; Mr.&nbsp;Bresky received a Bachelor of Arts
degree from the University of California &#150; Berkeley and a Masters of Business
Administration degree from the University of Texas at Austin.</font></p>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;text-autospace:none;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="font-size:10.0pt;margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><b><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-weight:bold;">M. Jason Mattox </font></b>is

and became a member of its Investment Committee in 1998.&#160; From 1999 until 2001, Mr.&nbsp;Mattox served
as Vice President of Sun Resorts International,&nbsp;Inc., a recreational
property investment company, coordinating marina acquisitions throughout the
southern United States and the U.S. Virgin Islands.&#160; From 1999 until 2001, in addition to
providing services related to investing, acquisition, disposition and

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:0in;"><b><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-weight:bold;">Other
Personnel</font></b></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:0in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;text-autospace:none;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">The
General Partners are assisted by the officers and employees of HPT, which is
the general partner of Behringer Advisors&nbsp;I.&#160; HPT and its affiliates employed approximately
370 full-time persons at December&nbsp;31, 2007, including the executive
officers listed above.&#160; HPT and its
affiliates will continue to hire employees as needed.&#160; HPT and its affiliates also will engage the
services of non-affiliated third parties to assist with the identification of
properties for possible acquisition and management of our operations.</font></p>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;text-autospace:none;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<h2 style="font-style:italic;font-weight:bold;margin:0in 0in .0001pt;page-break-after:avoid;"><b><i><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-style:normal;">Advisory Board</font></i></b></h2>

<h2 style="font-style:italic;font-weight:bold;margin:0in 0in .0001pt;page-break-after:avoid;"><b><i><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></i></b></h2>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;text-autospace:none;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">We do
not have a board of directors.&#160; The
General Partners are assisted by an advisory board.&#160; No member of the advisory board may be a
general partner, officer or employee of ours, Behringer Advisors I, or an
affiliate of us or Behringer Advisors&nbsp;I, although members of the advisory
board may purchase or own securities of, or have other business relations with,
such parties.&#160; The members of the
advisory board are Patrick M. Arnold, Ralph G. Edwards,&nbsp;Jr., Robert W.
McMillan and Scott F. McMullin.</font></p>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;text-autospace:none;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;text-autospace:none;text-indent:0in;"><b><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-weight:bold;">No Audit Committee; No &#147;Audit Committee Financial
Expert&#148;</font></b></p>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;text-autospace:none;text-indent:0in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;text-autospace:none;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">We do
not have a board of directors and, as such, have no board committees such as an
audit committee.&#160; Because we do not have
an audit committee, we do not have an &#147;audit committee financial expert.&#148;&#160; The General Partners are responsible for
managing the relationship with our Independent Registered Public Accounting
Firm.</font></p>

<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;text-autospace:none;text-indent:0in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;text-autospace:none;text-indent:0in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">42</font></p>

<div align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;text-autospace:none;text-indent:0in;"><hr size="3" width="100%" noshade color="#010101" align="left"></div>

<br clear="all" style="page-break-before:always;">


<div style="font-family:Times New Roman;">

<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;text-autospace:none;text-indent:0in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<h1 style="font-weight:bold;margin:0in 0in .0001pt;page-break-after:avoid;"><b><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">Section&nbsp;16(a)&nbsp;Beneficial
Ownership Reporting Compliance</font></b></h1>

<h1 style="font-weight:bold;margin:0in 0in .0001pt;page-break-after:avoid;"><b><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></b></h1>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;text-autospace:none;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">Section&nbsp;16(a)&nbsp;of
the Exchange Act, as amended, requires each director, officer, and individual
beneficially owning more than 10% of a registered security of the Partnership
to file with the SEC reports of security ownership and reports on subsequent
changes in ownership of their securities within specified time frames.&#160; These specified time frames require the
reporting of changes in ownership within two business days of the transaction
giving rise to the reporting obligation.&#160;
Reporting persons are required to furnish us with copies of all Section&nbsp;16(a)&nbsp;forms
filed with the SEC.&#160; Based upon our
review of the reports furnished to us pursuant to Section&nbsp;16(a)&nbsp;of
the Exchange Act, to the best of our knowledge, all required Section&nbsp;16(a)&nbsp;filings
were timely and correctly made by reporting persons during 2007.</font></p>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;text-autospace:none;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;text-autospace:none;text-indent:0in;"><b><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-weight:bold;">Code of Ethics</font></b></p>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;text-autospace:none;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;text-autospace:none;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">Behringer
Advisors I has adopted a code of ethics applicable to its principal executive
officer, principal financial officer, principal accounting officer, controller
and other employees.&#160; A copy of the code
of ethics of Behringer Advisors I may be obtained from our website at
http://www.behringerharvard.com.&#160; The web
site will be updated to include any material waivers or modifications to the
code of ethics.</font></p>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;text-autospace:none;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><b><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-weight:bold;">Item 11.&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160; Executive
Compensation.</font></b><a name="Item11_ExecutiveCompensation__181734"></a></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">We
operate under the direction of our General Partners, which are responsible for
the management and control of our affairs.&#160;
As of December&nbsp;31, 2007, we have not made any payments to Mr.&nbsp;Behringer
as compensation for serving as general partner.&#160;
The officers and employees of HPT assist the General Partners.&#160; The officers and employees of HPT do not
devote all of their time to managing us, and they do not receive any
compensation from us for their services.&#160;
We pay fees to Behringer Advisors I and its other affiliates, as
provided for in our Partnership Agreement.&#160;
Accordingly, we do not have, and our General Partners have not
considered, a compensation policy or program for themselves, their affiliates,
any employees of Behringer Advisors I or any employees of affiliates of our
General Partners and have not included a Compensation Discussion and Analysis
in this Annual Report on Form <br>
10-K.&nbsp;See Item 13. &#147;Certain Relationships and Related Transactions, and
Director Independence&#148; for a description of the fees payable and expenses
reimbursed to our affiliates.</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="font-size:10.0pt;margin:0in 0in .0001pt 1.0in;text-indent:-1.0in;"><b><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-weight:bold;">Item 12.</font></b><b><font size="1" style="font-size:3.0pt;font-weight:bold;">&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160; </font></b><b>Security Ownership of Certain
Beneficial Owners and Management and Related Stockholder Matters.</b><a name="Item12_SecurityOwnershipOfCertain_181738"></a></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt 1.0in;text-indent:-1.0in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">There
were no limited partners known by us who owned more than 5% of our limited
partnership units as of March&nbsp;1, 2008.</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">We
do not have any officers or directors.&#160;
Our two General Partners, Robert M. Behringer and Behringer Harvard
Advisors I, each own 50% of the general partnership interest.&#160; We do not maintain any equity compensation
plans, and no arrangements exist that would, upon operation, result in a change
in control for us.</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">The following table sets
forth information as of March&nbsp;1, 2008 regarding the beneficial ownership
of our limited partnership interest and general partnership interest by each
person known by us to own 5% or more of the outstanding class of partnership
interest, each of our directors or those of our General Partners, each of our
executive officers or those of our General Partners, and our directors and
executive officers, or those of our General Partners, as a group.&#160; The percentage of beneficial ownership is
calculated based on 4,309,444 limited partnership units and contributions from
our General Partners.</font></p>

<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt 9.0pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><b><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-weight:bold;">Item 13.&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160; Certain
Relationships and Related Transactions, and Director Independence.</font></b><a name="Item13_CertainRelationshipsAndRel_181742"></a></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;text-autospace:none;text-indent:0in;"><b><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-weight:bold;">Transactions with Related Persons</font></b></p>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;text-autospace:none;text-indent:0in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;text-autospace:none;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">The
General Partners and certain of their affiliates are entitled to receive fees
and compensation in connection with the acquisition, management and sale of our
assets, and have received fees in the past in connection with the Offering.&#160; Our General Partners have agreed that all of
the financial benefits of serving as our general partners will be allocated to
Behringer Advisors I since it is anticipated that the day-to-day
responsibilities of serving as our general partner will be performed by
Behringer Advisors I through the executive officers of its general partner.</font></p>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;text-autospace:none;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;text-autospace:none;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">During
the term of the Offering, Behringer Securities, our dealer manager and a
related party of our General Partners, received commissions of up to 7% of
gross offering proceeds before reallowance of commissions earned by
participating broker-dealers.&#160; In
addition, up to 2.5% of gross proceeds before reallowance to participating
broker-dealers was paid to Behringer Securities as a dealer manager fee; except
that this dealer manager fee was reduced to 1% of the gross proceeds of
purchases made pursuant to our DRIP, which was terminated with the termination
of the Offering on February&nbsp;19, 2005.&#160;
Behringer Securities reallowed all of its commissions to participating
broker-dealers and reallowed a portion of its dealer manager fee of up to 1.5%
of the gross offering proceeds to such participating broker-dealers as
marketing fees and due diligence expense reimbursement.&#160; Behringer Securities did not earn selling
commissions and dealer manager fees for the years ended December&nbsp;31, 2007
and December&nbsp;31, 2006 as a result of the termination of the Offering on February&nbsp;19,
2005.&#160; For the year ended December&nbsp;31,
2005, Behringer Securities earned $873,522 in selling commissions and $319,073
in dealer manager fees, which were recorded as a reduction in Partners&#146;
capital.</font></p>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;text-autospace:none;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">Behringer
Advisors I, our General Partner, or its affiliates received up to 2.5% of gross
offering proceeds for reimbursement of organization and offering expenses.&#160; As of December&nbsp;31, 2007, $1,097,713 of
organization and offering expenses were incurred by Behringer Advisors I on our
behalf, all which has been reimbursed.&#160;
Of the $1,097,713 of organization and offering costs reimbursed by us as
of December&nbsp;31, 2007, $1,077,517 was recorded as a reduction in Partners&#146;
capital and $20,196 was expensed as organization costs.&#160; For the years ended December&nbsp;31, 2007
and 2006, no additional organization or offering expenses were incurred or
reimbursed.&#160; For the year ended December&nbsp;31,
2005, we reimbursed $316,958 of organization and offering expenses, of which
$311,120 was recorded as a reduction in Partners&#146; capital and $5,838 was
expensed as organization costs.&#160;
Behringer Advisors I or its affiliates determined the amount of
organization and offering expenses owed, based on specific invoice
identification as well as an allocation of costs to us and other Behringer
Harvard programs, based on the respective equity offering results of those
entities in offering.&#160; No further
proceeds will be raised by us as a result of the termination of the Offering
and, as a result, we will not make any further reimbursements to Behringer
Advisors&nbsp;I for organization and offering expenses incurred or that may be
incurred in the future on our behalf.</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">Behringer
Advisors I or its affiliates receive acquisition and advisory fees of up to 3%
of the contract purchase price of each asset for the acquisition, development
or construction of real property or, with respect to any mortgage loan, up to
3% of the funds advanced for the making or purchase of a mortgage loan.&#160; Behringer Advisors I or its affiliates also
receive up to 0.5% of the contract purchase price of the assets acquired by us
for reimbursement of expenses related to making investments.&#160; During the years ended December&nbsp;31, 2007
and December&nbsp;31, 2006, Behringer Advisors I did not earn acquisition and
advisory fees and was not reimbursed for any acquisition-related expenses.&#160; During the year ended December&nbsp;31, 2005,
Behringer Advisors I earned $465,000 of acquisition and advisory fees and was
reimbursed $77,500 for acquisition-related expenses.</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">For
the management and leasing of our properties, we pay HPT Management, our
property manager and an affiliate of our General Partners, property management
and leasing fees equal to the lesser of:&#160;
(A)&nbsp;the amounts charged by unaffiliated persons rendering
comparable services in the same geographic area or (B)(1)&nbsp;for commercial
properties that are not leased on a long-term net lease basis, 4% of gross
revenues, plus separate leasing fees of up to 1.5% of gross revenues based upon
the customary leasing fees applicable to the geographic location of the
properties, and (2)&nbsp;in the case of commercial properties that are leased
on a long-term net lease basis (10 or more years), 1% of gross revenues plus a
one-time initial leasing fee of 3% of gross revenues payable over the first
five years of the lease term.&#160; We
reimburse the costs and expenses incurred by HPT Management on our behalf,
including the wages and salaries and other employee-related expenses of all
on-site employees of HPT Management who are engaged in the operation,
management, maintenance and leasing or access control of our properties,
including taxes, insurance and benefits relating to such employees, and legal, </font></p>

<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">44</font></p>

<div style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><hr size="3" width="100%" noshade color="#010101" align="left"></div>

</div>
<!-- SEQ.=1,FOLIO='44',FILE='C:\JMS\C900113\08-2957-1\task2822383\2957-1-ba-07.htm',USER='C900113',CD='Mar 29 09:05 2008' -->


<br clear="all" style="page-break-before:always;">


<div style="font-family:Times New Roman;">

<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">travel
and other out-of-pocket expenses that are directly related to the management of
specific properties.&#160; During the years
ended December&nbsp;31, 2007, 2006 and 2005, we incurred property management
fees payable to HPT Management of $157,638, $206,611 and $150,503,
respectively, of which $49,350 and $41,983, respectively, is included in income
from discontinued operations for the years ended December&nbsp;31, 2006 and
2005.</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">We
pay Behringer Advisors I, or its affiliates, an annual asset management fee of
0.5% of the contract purchase price of our assets.&#160; Any portion of the asset management fee may
be deferred and paid in a subsequent year.&#160;
During the year ended December&nbsp;31, 2007, we incurred asset
management fees of $140,271.&#160; During the
years ended December&nbsp;31, 2006 and 2005, we incurred asset management fees
of $166,057 and $130,878, respectively, of which $29,576 and $29,500,
respectively, are included in income from discontinued operations.</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">In
connection with the sale of our properties, we will pay Behringer Advisors I or
its affiliates a subordinated disposition fee in an amount not exceeding the
lesser of:&#160; (A)&nbsp;50% of the
reasonable, customary and competitive real estate brokerage commissions
customarily paid for the sale of a comparable property in light of the size,
type and location of the property, or (B)&nbsp;3% of the gross sales price of
each property, subordinated to distributions to limited partners from the sale
proceeds of an amount which, together with prior distributions to the limited
partners, will equal (1)&nbsp;100% of their capital contributions plus (2)&nbsp;an
8% annual cumulative (noncompounded) return of their net capital
contributions.&#160; Subordinated disposition
fees that are not payable at the date of sale, because limited partners have
not yet received their required minimum distributions, will be deferred and
paid at such time as these subordination conditions have been satisfied.&#160; In addition, after the limited partners have
received a return of their net capital contributions and an 8% annual
cumulative (noncompounded) return on their net capital contributions, then
Behringer Advisors I is entitled to receive 15% of the remaining residual
proceeds available for distribution (a subordinated participation in net sale
proceeds and distributions); provided, however, that in no event will the
General Partners receive in the aggregate more than 15% of sale proceeds
remaining after the limited partners have received a return of their net
capital contributions.&#160; Since the above
conditions have not been met at this time, we incurred no such real estate
commissions for the years ended December&nbsp;31, 2007, 2006 or 2005.</font></p>

<p style="font-size:10.0pt;margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;">&nbsp;</p>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;text-autospace:none;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">We are
dependent on Behringer Advisors I and HPT Management for certain services that
are essential to us, including asset acquisition and disposition decisions,
property management and leasing services and other general administrative
responsibilities.&#160; In the event that
these companies were unable to provide the respective services to us, we would
be required to obtain such services from other sources.</font></p>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;text-autospace:none;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><b><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-weight:bold;">Policies
and Procedures for Transactions with Related Persons</font></b></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">The
agreements and arrangements among us, our General Partners and their affiliates
have been established by our General Partners, and our General Partners believe
the amounts to be paid thereunder to be reasonable and customary under the
circumstances.&#160; In an effort to establish
standards for minimizing and resolving these potential conflicts of interest, our
General Partners have agreed to the guidelines and limitations set forth in our
Partnership Agreement.&#160; Among other
things, these provisions:</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p align="left" style="font-size:10.0pt;margin:0in 0in .0001pt .75in;text-align:left;text-autospace:none;text-indent:-.25in;"><font size="2" face="Symbol" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&#183;</font><font size="1" style="font-size:3.0pt;">&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160; </font>set forth the specific conditions
under which we may own or lease property jointly or in a partnership with an
affiliate of the General Partners;</p>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt .75in;text-align:left;text-autospace:none;text-indent:-.25in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p align="left" style="font-size:10.0pt;margin:0in 0in .0001pt .75in;text-align:left;text-autospace:none;text-indent:-.25in;"><font size="2" face="Symbol" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&#183;</font><font size="1" style="font-size:3.0pt;">&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160; </font>prohibit us from purchasing or
leasing an investment property from our General Partners or their affiliates
except under certain limited circumstances;</p>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt .75in;text-align:left;text-autospace:none;text-indent:-.25in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p align="left" style="font-size:10.0pt;margin:0in 0in .0001pt .75in;text-align:left;text-autospace:none;text-indent:-.25in;"><font size="2" face="Symbol" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&#183;</font><font size="1" style="font-size:3.0pt;">&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160; </font>prohibit loans by us to our General
Partners or their affiliates;</p>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt .75in;text-align:left;text-autospace:none;text-indent:-.25in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p align="left" style="font-size:10.0pt;margin:0in 0in .0001pt .75in;text-align:left;text-autospace:none;text-indent:-.25in;"><font size="2" face="Symbol" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&#183;</font><font size="1" style="font-size:3.0pt;">&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160; </font>prohibit the commingling of
partnership funds (except in the case of making capital contributions to joint
ventures and to the limited extent permissible under the NASAA Guidelines); and</p>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt .75in;text-align:left;text-autospace:none;text-indent:-.25in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p align="left" style="font-size:10.0pt;margin:0in 0in .0001pt .75in;text-align:left;text-autospace:none;text-indent:-.25in;"><font size="2" face="Symbol" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&#183;</font><font size="1" style="font-size:3.0pt;">&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160; </font>with certain exceptions, prohibit our
General Partners from merging or consolidating us with another partnership or a
corporation or converting us to a corporation unless the transaction complies
with certain terms and conditions including first obtaining a majority vote of
our limited partners.</p>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt 45.0pt;text-align:left;text-autospace:none;text-indent:-.25in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">In
addition, our General Partners have a fiduciary obligation to act in the best
interests of both our limited partners and the investors in other affiliated
programs and will use their best efforts to assure that we will be treated at
least as favorably as any other affiliated program.</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><b><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-weight:bold;">Item 14.&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160; Principal
Accounting Fees and Services.</font></b><a name="Item14_PrincipalAccountingFeesAnd_181750"></a></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;text-autospace:none;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">Because we do not have a board of
directors or any board committees, including an audit committee, the General
Partners pre-approve all auditing and permissible non-auditing services
provided by our independent registered public accounting firm.&#160; The independent public accountants may not be
retained to perform the non-auditing services specified in Section&nbsp;10A(g)&nbsp;of
the Exchange Act.</font></p>

<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;text-autospace:none;text-indent:0in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;text-autospace:none;text-indent:0in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">45</font></p>

<div align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;text-autospace:none;text-indent:0in;"><hr size="3" width="100%" noshade color="#010101" align="left"></div>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;text-autospace:none;text-indent:0in;"><b><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-weight:bold;">Fees Paid to Independent Registered Public Accounting Firm</font></b></p>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;text-autospace:none;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<div style="font-family:Times New Roman;">

<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;"><b><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-weight:bold;">REPORT OF INDEPENDENT REGISTERED PUBLIC ACCOUNTING FIRM</font></b><a name="ReportOfIndependentRegisteredPubl_204040"></a></p>

<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">To the Partners of</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">Behringer Harvard Mid-Term Value Enhancement
Fund I LP</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">Addison, Texas</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="font-size:10.0pt;margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">We have audited the accompanying consolidated
balance sheets of the </font>Behringer
Harvard Mid-Term Value Enhancement Fund I LP and subsidiaries (the &#147;Partnership&#148;)
as of December&nbsp;31, 2007 and 2006
and the related consolidated statements of operations, partner&#146;s capital
and cash flows for each of the three
years in the period ended December&nbsp;31, 2007.&#160; These financial statements are the
responsibility of the Partnership&#146;s management.&nbsp; Our responsibility is to
express an opinion on these financial statements based on our audits.</p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">We conducted our audits in accordance with
the standards of the Public Company Accounting Oversight Board (United
States).&nbsp; Those standards require that we plan and perform the audits to
obtain reasonable assurance about whether the financial statements are free of
material misstatement.&nbsp; The Partnership is not required to have, nor were
we engaged to perform, an audit of its internal control over financial
reporting.&nbsp; Our audits included consideration of internal control over
financial reporting as a basis for designing audit procedures that are
appropriate in the circumstances, but not for the purpose of expressing an
opinion on the effectiveness of the Partnership&#146;s internal control over
financial reporting.&nbsp; Accordingly, we express no such opinion.&nbsp; An
audit also includes examining, on a test basis, evidence supporting the amounts
and disclosures in the financial statements, assessing the accounting
principles used and significant estimates made by management, as well as evaluating
the overall financial statement presentation.&nbsp; We believe that our audits
provide a reasonable basis for our opinion.</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">In our opinion, such consolidated financial
statements present fairly, in all material respects, the financial position of
the Partnership at December&nbsp;31, 2007 and 2006, and the results of its
operations and its cash flows for each of the three years in the period ended December&nbsp;31,
2007, in conformity with accounting principles generally accepted in the United
States of America.</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman">&nbsp;</font></p>

  <td width="1%" valign="bottom" bgcolor="#CCEEFF" style="background:#CCEEFF;border:none;border-bottom:double windowtext 1.5pt;padding:0in 0in 0in 0in;width:1.12%;">
  <p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">$</font></p>
  </td>
  <td width="9%" valign="bottom" bgcolor="#CCEEFF" style="background:#CCEEFF;border-bottom:double windowtext 1.5pt;border-left:none;border-right:none;border-top:solid windowtext 1.0pt;padding:0in 0in 0in 0in;width:9.88%;">
  <p align="right" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:right;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">7,613,543</font></p>
  </td>
  <td width="2%" valign="bottom" bgcolor="#CCEEFF" style="background:#CCEEFF;padding:0in 0in 0in 0in;width:2.5%;">

<br clear="all" style="page-break-before:always;">
<div>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;"><b><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-weight:bold;">Behringer Harvard Mid-Term Value Enhancement Fund I LP</font></b></p>

<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;"><b><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-weight:bold;">Notes to Consolidated Financial
Statements</font></b><a name="NotesToConsolidatedFinancialState_204211"></a></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><b><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-weight:bold;">1.&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160; Business and
Organization</font></b></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt .25in;"><b><i><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-style:italic;font-weight:bold;">Business</font></i></b></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt .25in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">Behringer
Harvard Mid-Term Value Enhancement Fund I LP is a limited partnership formed in
Texas on July&nbsp;30, 2002.&#160; Our general
partners are Behringer Harvard Advisors I LP (&#147;Behringer Advisors I&#148;) and
Robert M. Behringer (collectively, the &#147;General Partners&#148;).&#160; We were funded through capital contributions
from our General Partners and initial limited partner on September&nbsp;20,
2002 (date of inception) and offered our limited partnership units pursuant to
the public offering which commenced on February&nbsp;19, 2003 (the &#147;Offering&#148;)
and terminated on February&nbsp;19, 2005.&#160;
The Offering was a best efforts continuous offering and we admitted new
investors until the termination of the Offering in February&nbsp;2005.</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">We
used the proceeds from the Offering, after deducting offering expenses,
primarily to acquire six office building properties.&#160; We are not currently seeking to purchase any
additional properties for our portfolio; however, in limited circumstances, we
may purchase properties as a result of selling one or more of the properties we
currently hold and reinvesting the sales proceeds in properties that fall
within our investment objectives and investment criteria.&#160; We are not limited to investments in
institutional quality office properties.&#160;
We may invest in other commercial properties, such as shopping centers,
business and industrial parks, manufacturing facilities, warehouse and distribution
facilities, in order to reduce overall portfolio risk or enhance overall
portfolio returns if our General Partners determine that it would be
advantageous to do so.&#160; In addition, our
General Partners may determine that it would be advantageous to acquire
commercial properties other than institutional quality office properties in
order to diversify our portfolio or in order to respond to changes in the real
estate market.&#160; We may also invest in
commercial properties that are not preleased to such tenants or in other types
of commercial properties such as hotels or motels.</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">We
may purchase properties that have been constructed and have operating
histories, are newly constructed or are under development or construction.&#160; An advisory board has been established to
provide the General Partners with advice and guidance with respect to (1)&nbsp;the
identification of assets for acquisition; (2)&nbsp;general economic and market
conditions, general business principles, and specific business principles
relating to our business plan; (3)&nbsp;inroads to establishing beneficial
strategic partners, customers, and suppliers; (4)&nbsp;opportunities within and
related to the industry; and (5)&nbsp;other assistance as may be determined by
the General Partners or their representatives from time to time.&#160; Our partnership agreement (the &#147;Partnership
Agreement&#148;) provides that we will continue in existence until the earlier of December&nbsp;31,
2022 or termination of the Partnership pursuant to the dissolution and
termination provisions of the Partnership Agreement.</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt .25in;"><b><i><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-style:italic;font-weight:bold;">Organization</font></i></b></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt .25in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">On
February&nbsp;19, 2003, we commenced the Offering of up to 40,000,000 limited
partnership units offered at a price of $10 per unit pursuant to a Registration
Statement on Form&nbsp;S-11 (the &#147;Registration Statement&#148;) filed under the
Securities Act of 1933.&#160; The Registration
Statement also covered up to 4,000,000 limited partnership units available
pursuant to our distribution reinvestment and automatic purchase plan (the &#147;DRIP&#148;)
at $10 per unit.&#160; We terminated our DRIP
on February&nbsp;19, 2005 with the termination of the Offering.</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">As
of December&nbsp;31, 2007 and 2006, we had 4,309,444 limited partnership units
outstanding.&#160; Our limited partnership
units are not currently listed on a national exchange, and we do not expect any
public market for the units to develop.</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><b><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-weight:bold;">2.&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160; Summary of
Significant Accounting Policies</font></b></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.25in;"><b><i><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-style:italic;font-weight:bold;">Use of Estimates in the Preparation of Financial
Statements</font></i></b></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">The
preparation of financial statements in conformity with accounting principles
generally accepted in the United States of America requires management to make
estimates and assumptions that affect the reported amounts of assets and
liabilities and disclosure of contingent assets and liabilities at the date of
the financial statements and the reported amounts of revenues and expenses
during the reporting period.&#160; These
estimates include such items as purchase price allocation for real estate
acquisitions, impairment of long-lived assets, depreciation and amortization
and allowance for doubtful accounts.&#160;
Actual results could differ from those estimates.</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.25in;"><b><i><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-style:italic;font-weight:bold;">Principles of Consolidation and Basis of
Presentation</font></i></b></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.25in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">The
consolidated financial statements include our accounts and the accounts of all
of our subsidiaries.&#160; All inter-company
transactions, balances and profits have been eliminated in consolidation.&#160; Interests in entities acquired are evaluated
based on Financial Accounting Standards Board Interpretation (&#147;FIN&#148;) No.&nbsp;46R
&#147;Consolidation of Variable Interest Entities,&#148; which requires the consolidation
of variable interest entities (&#147;VIE&#148;) in which we are deemed to be the primary
beneficiary.&#160; If the interest in the
entity is determined not to be a VIE under FIN No.&nbsp;46R, then the entities
are</font></p>

<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">F-7</font></p>

<div style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><hr size="3" width="100%" noshade color="#010101" align="left"></div>

</div>
<!-- SEQ.=1,FOLIO='F-7',FILE='C:\JMS\c900126\08-2957-1\task2825051\2957-1-ba-11.htm',USER='c900126',CD='Mar 30 23:24 2008' -->


<br clear="all" style="page-break-before:always;">


<div>

<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;"><b><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-weight:bold;">Behringer Harvard Mid-Term Value Enhancement Fund I LP</font></b></p>

<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;"><b><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-weight:bold;">Notes to Consolidated Financial Statements</font></b></p>

<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">evaluated for consolidation under the
American Institute of Certified Public Accountants (&#147;AICPA&#148;) Statement of
Position (&#147;SOP&#148;) 78-9 &#147;Accounting for Investments in Real Estate Ventures,&#148; as
amended by Emerging Issues Task Force (&#147;EITF&#148;) Issue 04-5, &#147;Determining Whether
a General Partner, or the General Partners as a Group, Controls a Limited
Partnership or Similar Entity When the Limited Partners Have Certain Rights.&#148;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.25in;"><b><i><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-style:italic;font-weight:bold;">Real Estate</font></i></b></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.25in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">Upon
the acquisition of real estate properties, we allocate the purchase price of
those properties to the tangible assets acquired, consisting of land, inclusive
of associated rights, and buildings, identified intangible assets, asset
retirement obligations and assumed liabilities based on their relative fair
values in accordance with Statement of Financial Accounting Standards No.&nbsp;142,
&#147;Goodwill and Other Intangible Assets.&#148;&#160;
Identified intangible assets consist of the fair value of above-market
and below-market leases, in-place leases, in-place tenant improvements and
tenant relationships.&#160; Initial valuations
are subject to change until our information is finalized, which is no later
than 12 months from the acquisition date.</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">The
fair value of the tangible assets acquired, consisting of land and buildings,
is determined by valuing the property as if it were vacant, and the &#147;as-if-vacant&#148;
value is then allocated to land and buildings.&#160;
Land values are derived from appraisals, and building values are
calculated as replacement cost less depreciation or management&#146;s estimates of
the relative fair value of these assets using discounted cash flow analyses or
similar methods.&#160; The value of buildings
is depreciated over the estimated useful life of 25 years using the straight-line
method.</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">We
determine the value of above-market and below-market in-place leases for
acquired properties based on the present value (using an interest rate that
reflects the risks associated with the leases acquired) of the difference
between (1)&nbsp;the contractual amounts to be paid pursuant to the in-place
leases and (2)&nbsp;management&#146;s estimate of current market lease rates for the
corresponding in-place leases, measured over a period equal to the determined
lease term.&#160; We record the fair value of
above-market and below-market leases as intangible assets or intangible
liabilities, respectively, and amortize them as an adjustment to rental income
over the determined lease term.</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">The
total value of identified real estate intangible assets acquired is further
allocated to in-place lease values, in-place tenant improvements, in-place
leasing commissions and tenant relationships based on management&#146;s evaluation
of the specific characteristics of each tenant&#146;s lease and our overall
relationship with that respective tenant.&#160;
The aggregate value for tenant improvements and leasing commissions is
based on estimates of these costs incurred at inception of the acquired leases,
amortized through the date of acquisition.&#160;
The aggregate value of in-place leases acquired and tenant relationships
is determined by applying a fair value model.&#160;
The estimates of fair value of in-place leases includes an estimate of
carrying costs during the expected lease-up periods for the respective spaces
considering current market conditions.&#160;
In estimating the carrying costs that would have otherwise been incurred
had the leases not been in place, management includes such items as real estate
taxes, insurance and other operating expenses as well as lost rental revenue
during the expected lease-up period based on current market conditions.&#160; The estimates of fair value of tenant
relationships also include costs to execute similar leases including leasing
commissions, legal and tenant improvements as well as an estimate of the
likelihood of renewal as determined by management on a tenant-by-tenant basis.</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">We
amortize the value of in-place leases and in-place tenant improvements to
expense over the initial term of the respective leases.&#160; The value of tenant relationship intangibles
is amortized to expense over the initial term and any anticipated renewal
periods, but in no event does the amortization period for intangible assets
exceed the remaining depreciable life of the building.&#160; Should a tenant terminate its lease, the
unamortized portion of the related real estate intangibles would be charged to
expense.&#160; The estimated useful lives for
lease intangibles range from 1.2 years to 9.3 years.</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in .05in .0001pt 0in;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">Anticipated amortization for each of the following
five years ended December&nbsp;31 and thereafter is as follows:</font></p>

<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

  <td width="2%" valign="bottom" bgcolor="#CCEEFF" style="background:#CCEEFF;padding:0in 0in 0.375pt 0in;width:2.88%;">
  <p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">)</font></p>
  </td>
  <td width="12%" colspan="2" valign="bottom" bgcolor="#CCEEFF" style="background:#CCEEFF;border:none;border-bottom:solid windowtext 1.0pt;padding:0in 0in 0in 0in;width:12.7%;">
  <p align="right" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:right;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">147,141</font></p>
  </td>

  </td>

  <td width="52%" valign="top" style="padding:0in 0in 0in 0in;width:52.12%;">

  </td>
  <td width="2%" valign="bottom" style="padding:0in 0in 0in 0in;width:2.88%;">

  <p align="right" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:right;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">16,575,754</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in .05in .0001pt 0in;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.25in;"><b><i><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-style:italic;font-weight:bold;">Impairment of Long-Lived Assets</font></i></b></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.25in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">For
real estate we wholly-own, management monitors events and changes in
circumstances indicating that the carrying amounts of the real estate assets
may not be recoverable.&#160; When such events
or changes in circumstances occur, we assess potential impairment by comparing
estimated future undiscounted operating cash flows expected to be generated
over the life of the asset, including its eventual disposition, to the carrying
amount of the asset.&#160; In the event that
the carrying amount exceeds the estimated future undiscounted operating cash
flows, we perform an impairment analysis to determine if the carrying amount of
the asset needs to be reduced to estimated fair value.&#160; We determine the estimated fair value based
on cash flow streams using various factors including estimated future selling
prices, costs spent to date, remaining budgeted costs and selling costs.</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">During
2007, the housing market experienced difficult conditions including high
inventory levels, tightening of the credit market and weak consumer
confidence.&#160; In the event that market
conditions continue to decline in the future or the current difficult market
conditions extend beyond our expectations, impairments may be necessary in the
future.&#160; There were no impairment charges
for long-lived assets for the years ended December&nbsp;31, 2007, 2006 or 2005.</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.25in;"><b><i><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-style:italic;font-weight:bold;">Cash and Cash Equivalents</font></i></b></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.25in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">We
consider investments in highly-liquid money market funds with original maturities
of three months or less to be cash equivalents.</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.25in;"><b><i><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-style:italic;font-weight:bold;">Restricted Cash</font></i></b></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.25in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">Restricted
cash is comprised of monies held in an escrow account relating to a property
sold in December&nbsp;2006.&#160; During the
year ended December&nbsp;31, 2006, the monies in this account were held to
complete a like-kind exchange.&#160; During
the year ended December&nbsp;31, 2007, a like-kind exchange did not occur and
the funds were transferred to operating cash.</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.25in;"><b><i><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-style:italic;font-weight:bold;">Accounts Receivable</font></i></b></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.25in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">Accounts
receivable primarily consists of receivables from tenants related to those
properties that are consolidated in our financial statements.&#160; We recorded an allowance for doubtful
accounts associated with accounts receivable of $8,267 and $1,675 at December&nbsp;31,
2007 and 2006, respectively.</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.25in;"><b><i><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-style:italic;font-weight:bold;">Prepaid Expenses and Other Assets</font></i></b></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.25in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">Prepaid
expenses and other assets include prepaid directors&#146; and officers&#146; insurance as
well as prepaid insurance for our real estate properties.</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.25in;"><b><i><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-style:italic;font-weight:bold;">Revenue Recognition</font></i></b></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.25in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;text-autospace:none;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">We recognize rental income generated from leases on
real estate assets on the straight-line basis over the terms of the respective
leases, including the effect of rent holidays, if any.&#160; For the years ended December&nbsp;31, 2007,
2006 and 2005, the total net increases to rental revenues due to straight-line
rent adjustments were $55,534, $25,492 and $36,327, respectively.&#160; Any payments made to tenants that are
considered lease incentives or inducements are being amortized to revenue over
the life of the respective leases.</font></p>

<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;text-autospace:none;text-indent:0in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;text-autospace:none;text-indent:0in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">F-9</font></p>

<div align="left" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:left;text-autospace:none;text-indent:0in;"><hr size="3" width="100%" noshade color="#010101" align="left"></div>
